NOVO-B.CO.name: Novo Nordisk B
bull: GLP-1 demand (Ozempic/Wegovy) remains structurally intact with multi-year pipeline capacity expanding.
0.63% move above prev_close signals continued buying pressure after recent consolidation.
Strong FCF and dividend growth history supports institutional accumulation.
bear: Competitive pressure from Eli Lilly's tirzepatide continues to erode market share narrative.
DKK strength vs. USD could weigh on reported earnings.
Stock near lower end of 52-week range — momentum is fragile.
NOVO-B.CO.summary: Modest uptick suggests stabilization; GLP-1 secular tailwind keeps bias bullish despite competitive headwinds.

MAERSK-B.CO.name: Maersk B
bull: 1.59% is the strongest mover today — container freight rates showing signs of recovery after multi-quarter trough.
Cost-cutting and logistics diversification are improving margin resilience.
Red Sea/Suez rerouting continues to inflate ton-mile demand, keeping spot rates elevated.
bear: Global trade slowdown risk from US tariff escalation could compress volumes through H2 2026.
Overcapacity still entering the market (new vessel deliveries) — structural ceiling on rates.
High absolute price (14,990 DKK) creates large drawdown risk on negative macro news.
MAERSK-B.CO.summary: Strongest daily gain of the three; freight rate recovery and rerouting tailwinds outweigh overcapacity risk near-term.

DSV.CO.name: DSV
bull: DSV's DB Schenker integration continues to build scale — largest logistics player in Europe.
Margin expansion story intact as synergies from acquisition are realized over 2026-2027.
Small positive move on a quiet day shows resilience — not selling off.
bear: 0.39% is the weakest performer today, suggesting relative underperformance vs. peers.
Integration execution risk remains elevated — DB Schenker is a massive bolt-on.
Slowing European manufacturing output (Germany) is a headwind for B2B freight volumes.
DSV.CO.summary: Integration upside is real but priced in; weak relative performance today warrants a wait-and-see stance.
